texto: I'm not good, of course; I wouldn't give a fig to be good. So it's not vanity. It's on a far grander scale; a splendid selfishness, - authorized, too; and papa and mamma brought me up to worship beauty, -and there's the fifth commandment, you know.

frase_original: I'm not good, of course; I wouldn't give a fig to be good. So it's not vanity. It's on a far grander scale; a splendid selfishness, - authorized, too; and papa and mamma brought me up to worship beauty, -and there's the fifth commandment, you know.

texto: A full moon is poison to some; they shut it out at every crevice, and do not suffer a ray to cross them; it has a chemical or magical effect; it sickens them. But I am never more free and royal than when the subtile celerity of its magic combinations, whatever they are, is at work.
explicacao: Citação de Harriet Prescott Spofford sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “A full moon is poison to some; they shut it out at every crevice, and do not suffer a ray to cros…” articula reflexao, moon, poison em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e reflexao.

seo.descriptionSeo: Citação de Harriet Prescott Spofford sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “A full moon is poison to some; they shut it out at every crevice, and do not suffe…

frase_original: A full moon is poison to some; they shut it out at every crevice, and do not suffer a ray to cross them; it has a chemical or magical effect; it sickens them. But I am never more free and royal than when the subtile celerity of its magic combinations, whatever they are, is at work.

texto: I endured all our hardships as if they had been luxuries: I made light of scurvy, banqueted off train-oil, and met that cold for which there is no language framed, and which might be a new element; or which, rather, had seemed in that long night like the vast void of ether beyond the uttermost star, where was neither air nor light nor heat, but only bitter negation and emptiness. I was hardly conscious of my body; I was only a concentrated search in myself.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Harriet Prescott Spofford: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a autoestima. O trecho “I endured all our hardships as if they had been luxuries: I made light of scurvy, banqueted off t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, cold, space pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
